articles.title: Did Kate Middleton Wear the Same Outfit as Princess Diana at Trooping the Colour?
articles.content:
穿和别人一样的衣服总是有点风险：他们穿得比你好看吗？人们会怎么说？如果你是一位皇室成员，还要面对公众和狂热的英国媒体的额外审视——尤其是当第一个穿这套衣服的人是已故的、伟大的、非常时尚的戴安娜王妃。

凯特·米德尔顿对这些情绪并不陌生（或流言蜚语）。这位威尔士王妃上周末在皇家阅兵仪式上亮相，穿着一件蓝白相间的连衣裙，让人立刻联想到她已故婆婆的经典造型。这场每年六月举办的庆祝君主生日的活动，让凯特、威廉和其他皇室成员齐聚白金汉宫阳台，观看由1400名士兵、200匹马和400名音乐家参与的盛大游行。

那么，你看过这两件裙子吗？你觉得怎么样？接下来，我们将带你了解这位王妃周末亮相后一直萦绕在人们心头的时尚话题。继续阅读，了解凯特今年的裙子究竟是模仿戴安娜的同款，还是一份充满爱意的致敬。

不，这不是同一件裙子，但你可能会误以为是，因为两件衣服非常相似。都是双排扣的浅蓝色凯瑟琳·沃克外套式连衣裙，配有白色滚边，只是蓝色略有不同。凯特的纽扣包有布料，而戴安娜的没有，翻领设计、口袋和滚边也略有不同。

大家都在讨论凯特的裙子模仿了戴安娜1987年复活节的造型，但还有另一套戴安娜和凯瑟琳·沃克的组合可能也会让你有似曾相识的感觉：1992年戴安娜访问印度德里时穿的浅蓝白色套装。虽然那次戴安娜穿的是白色百褶裙，但蓝色定制外套配白色滚边的设计与凯特在皇家阅兵仪式上的连衣裙惊人地相似。

既然没有华丽帽子的皇室装扮是不完整的，凯特今年的选择——浅蓝色和白色相间，宽帽檐——似乎也是为了呼应戴安娜类似风格的帽子。

我们无法确定，但这肯定不是凯特的时尚选择第一次让我们想起戴安娜。2022年，这位王妃身穿亚历山德拉·里奇设计的波点套装出席皇家阿斯科特赛马会，明显是在致敬戴安娜1986年出席埃普索姆赛马会时穿的维克多·埃德尔斯坦礼服。

说到波点，两位王妃在向公众介绍他们的第一个孩子时也都穿过波点服装。1982年，戴安娜抱着婴儿威廉穿着一件绿色波点连衣裙，而凯特2013年介绍乔治王子时穿了一件类似的、更现代的浅蓝色服装。同样，2018年凯特展示路易王子时穿了一件红色连衣裙配白色领口，呼应了戴安娜1984年抱着哈里王子时穿的那套。

皇室内部人士长期以来一直表示，威廉和凯特深知戴安娜作为时尚偶像的声誉，并暗示凯特的一些服装选择有时是刻意为之。《每日电讯报》时尚新闻与专题总监贝桑·霍尔特在接受《人物》采访时表示：“我认为我们已经看到很多例子表明这种致敬是非常有意的，我认为凯特利用时尚以一种非常积极的方式向戴安娜致敬。”

1997年，是威廉建议戴安娜将她的服装拿去拍卖做慈善。她照做了，为癌症和艾滋病研究筹集了超过300万美元。

凯特在今年的皇家阅兵仪式上不仅用裙子唤起了戴安娜的情怀——她的珠宝选择也引人注目。眼尖的皇室观察者立刻注意到了凯特的耳环：这是她2018年在路易王子洗礼仪式上首次佩戴的同款卡桑德拉·戈德珍珠花朵绽放耳环。今年早些时候，当凯特出席坎特伯雷大主教就职典礼时，这对漂亮的耳环再次出现。

最后，还有凯特上周末佩戴的手镯：一款三串珍珠手链，曾经属于戴安娜。这位已故王妃在20世纪80年代末多次佩戴它，最著名的一次是搭配一件被称为她“猫王”礼服的白色镶珠礼服，她当时佩戴的是标志性的情人结王冠。

威廉在他母亲去世后将这条手链送给了妻子，而这个周末，凯特自豪地佩戴着它。一个温馨的巧合是，威廉和凯特的女儿夏洛特公主似乎也戴着同款的奈杰尔·米尔恩设计手链的复制品。
articles.summary:
Wearing the same outfit as someone else always carries a bit of risk: Do they wear it better than you? What will people say? And if you’re a royal, there’s the extra scrutiny of the public and the rabid British press—especially if the person who first wore the look is the late, great and oh-so-fashionable Princess Diana.

Kate Middleton is no stranger to these emotions (or the whispers). The Princess of Wales stepped out this past weekend at Trooping the Colour wearing a blue-and-white dress that immediately called to mind a look made famous by her late mother-in-law. The event, held every June to celebrate the monarch’s birthday, brought together Kate, William and other royals on the Buckingham Palace balcony to watch a parade complete with 1,400 soldiers, 200 horses and 400 musicians.

So have you seen both dresses? What do you think? Ahead, we’ll take you through the latest fashion question lingering after the princess’ weekend appearance. Read on to learn whether Kate’s dress this year was a dupe for Diana’s or a loving homage.

Get Reader’s Digest’s Read Up newsletter for more royals, humor, travel, tech and fun facts all week long.

Did Kate wear the same dress as Diana once did?

No, it’s not the same dress, but you’d be forgiven for doing a double take because the two are remarkably similar. Both are double-breasted, light blue Catherine Walker coat dresses with white trim, though the blues are slightly different hues. Kate’s buttons are fabric-covered while Diana’s were not, and the lapel design, pockets and trim differ a bit as well.

Everyone is talking about Kate’s dress mimicking Diana’s Easter 1987 outfit, but there’s another Diana and Catherine Walker combo that might give you déjà vu as well: the light-blue-and-white suit Diana wore on a trip to Delhi, India, in 1992. Though Diana wore a white pleated skirt on that trip, the tailored blue jacket with white piping bears a striking resemblance to Kate’s Trooping the Colour dress.

And since no royal outfit would be complete without a fancy hat, Kate’s choice this year—light blue and white with a wide brim—also seems designed to recall Diana’s similarly styled lids.

What message was Kate sending with this outfit?

We can’t know for sure, but it’s definitely not the first time Kate’s fashion choices have given us Diana flashbacks. In 2022, the princess wore a polka-dot ensemble by Alessandra Rich to the Royal Ascot horse race, a clear nod to the Victor Edelstein dress that Diana wore to the Epsom Derby in 1986.

Speaking of polka dots, both princesses also wore spots when introducing their firstborn sons to the public. In 1982, Diana stepped out with baby William wearing a green polka-dot dress, while Kate sported a similar, if more modern, light-blue look in 2013 when introducing Prince George. Likewise, in 2018, Kate showed off Prince Louis in a red dress with a white collar, mirroring one Diana wore with Prince Harry in 1984.

Royal insiders have long said that William and Kate are aware of Diana’s reputation as a style icon and have hinted that Kate’s outfits are sometimes chosen deliberately with that in mind. “I think we have seen lots of examples where the reference is very intentional, and I think that Kate uses fashion to pay tribute to Diana in a very positive way,” Bethan Holt, the Daily Telegraph‘s fashion news and features director, told People.

In 1997, it was William who suggested that Diana auction her wardrobe off for charity. She did, raising more than $3 million for cancer and AIDS research.

Did Kate’s accessories also have special significance?

Not only did Kate’s dress evoke Diana nostalgia at this year’s Trooping the Colour—her jewelry choices also turned heads. Eagle-eyed royal watchers noticed Kate’s earrings right away: They were the same Cassandra Goad pearl floral burst earrings she first wore in 2018 for Louis’s christening. And earlier this year, the gorgeous earrings showed up again when Kate was on hand for the installation of the new archbishop of Canterbury.

Finally, there’s the bracelet Kate was wearing this weekend: a three-strand pearl stunner that used to be Diana’s. The late princess wore it multiple times in the late 1980s, most famously with a white bejeweled outfit known as her “Elvis” gown, which she wore with the iconic Lover’s Knot tiara.

William gifted the bracelet to his wife after his mom died, and this weekend, Kate wore it with pride. In a sweet twist, William and Kate’s daughter, Princess Charlotte, was wearing what looked to be a replica of the Nigel Milne–designed bracelet as well.

We regularly cover topics including the latest royal news, the history and meaning behind time-honored traditions, and the everyday quirks of everyone’s favorite family members, from Queen Elizabeth’s daily snack to Prince William’s confessions about his home life. articles.title: Always Take a Picture of This Room Before Leaving the House (Hint: It’s Not the One You Probably Think!)
articles.content:
我们离开家时通常有两种状态：稍微匆忙或稍微焦虑。无论哪种情况，出门的流程通常都一样——快速检查、反复确认，以及一种挥之不去的感觉——你可能忘了什么重要的东西。

我们大多数人都知道要关掉炉子、关上窗户、锁好门。但有一个简单的习惯可以让你的出门流程更加安心。不仅如此，如果你在外出时出了什么问题，它还能产生真正有用的东西。而这只需要拍一张照片。

我与保罗·加顿交谈过，他是一位持证房屋检查员和安全专家，创办了格雷波因特策略公司。我从他那里了解到，在你离开家时能让你安心的一个简单步骤。继续阅读，了解该怎么做以及什么时候做。

事实证明，最有用的“离家前”照片不是厨房、门廊或客厅，而是浴室。乍一听这可能让人惊讶，但根据加顿的说法，这个经常被忽视的空间在出门前值得关注的背后有一个实际原因。

“人们往往将注意力集中在风暴、入室行窃或外部发生的事情上，”加顿说。“但许多最严重的问题其实始于室内。”而很少有房间能像浴室一样集中这么多潜在的内部问题。

一张快速的照片将有助于在你离开前预防潜在问题，并记录房间清晰的“之前”状态，以防在你外出时发生意外。以下是一张简单的照片可以帮助预防和记录的事项：

从本质上讲，浴室是一个压力水管、排水系统、密封件和固定装置的网络——所有这些都隐藏在墙壁内和地板下。当一切正常运行时，你几乎注意不到它。当某个东西出问题时，它可能会迅速损坏。

“到处都是管道，而管道是许多家庭灾难的源头——尤其是水损害，”加顿说。

拍摄浴室照片会创建一个带时间戳的记录，如果你需要为保险目的记录损坏情况。通过为空间发生问题前的状态提供清晰的参考点，你的照片可以帮助确定发生了什么变化以及何时发生的。

“如果你回家时发现漏水或污渍在离开前并不存在，你现在有了证据，”加顿解释道。

加顿说，浴室往往比人们意识到的更有价值。除了固定装置本身，还有经常使用的高档物品：电动牙刷、美发工具、护肤品设备、化妆品和小家电，这些在匆忙中很容易被忽视……如果损坏，替换起来也很昂贵。

每次拍照时，你都会对自己的习惯有更好的了解，以及下次什么会更有效。也许你从未注意到每天白天都把吹风机插着，直到做晚间护肤的时候。离开时拍照可以促使你养成更好、更安全的习惯——出门前拔掉吹风机。

加顿建议从广角镜头开始，然后记录任何特别有价值的东西。在拍摄那张重要的浴室照片之前，有几件事是必须做的。以下是关注的重点：

洗衣房和附近的设备区是另一个常见的问题来源。

洗衣机软管即使在机器不运行时也保持压力状态，随着时间的推移，这些软管可能会老化或损坏。“如果软管在你外出时爆裂，没有任何东西能阻止水流出，”加顿说。“水会一直流。”对于较长的旅行，他建议完全关闭电器的供水阀。

热水器是另一个被忽视的风险。如果储水箱在房屋空置时发生故障，损害可能会迅速升级。“你面临的是30到70加仑的水立即释放，”加顿指出。“如果它继续尝试重新注水，就会不断加剧问题。”

除了洪水外，热水会在密闭空间产生蒸汽损坏，导致持续的潮湿、气味甚至霉菌问题。为增加保护，一些房主安装了与智能手机应用连接的经济型水传感器，实时提醒他们注意漏水。

拍照不一定要成为日常出门流程的一部分。相反，它在你离开超过24小时或外出时间足够长以至于不会立即注意到问题时最有用。

“我总是告诉人们拍照，或者更好的是做快速视频巡视，”加顿说。“视频能捕捉你可能没想到要拍照的东西，比如细微的漏水、电器的声音或空间的一般状况。如果你以后需要比较，它能给你更完整的画面。”

理想情况下，你的照片或视频应该存放在手机上的专用相册中——比如“旅行前的家”——这样需要时容易找到，而不是埋在相机胶卷里。这只需要不到一分钟。如果一切正常，它很快就会被遗忘。但如果出了问题，它可能是你拍过的最有用的照片之一。

关于专家

在读者文摘，我们致力于由具有专业知识和经验的作家制作高质量内容，并咨询相关合格专家。我们依赖政府、专业组织和学术机构等权威主要来源，以及作者在适当情况下的个人经历。我们验证所有事实和数据，用可靠来源支持，并随着时间推移重新审视，确保它们保持准确和最新。了解更多关于我们团队、贡献者和编辑政策的信息。

这篇文章首次出现在读者文摘上：《离开家前一定要拍这个房间的照片（提示：不是你可能想到的那个！）》
articles.summary:
We tend to leave home in two modes: slightly rushed or slightly paranoid. Either way, the exit routine usually looks the same—quick checks, double checks and a lingering sense that you might be forgetting something important.

Most of us know to turn off the stove, close the windows and lock the doors. But there’s one simple habit that can make your routine even more reassuring. Not only that, but it can produce something genuinely useful if things go wrong while you’re away. And all it involves is taking a photo.

I spoke with Paul Grattan, a licensed home inspector and security expert who founded Graypoint Strategies, to learn more about the easy step you can take to bring you peace of mind when you leave your home. Read on for what to do and when.

Get Reader’s Digest’s Read Up newsletter for more home tips, cleaning, travel, tech and fun facts all week long.

Which room should you take a picture of before leaving the house?

It turns out the most useful “before you leave” photo isn’t the kitchen, entryway or living room. It’s the bathroom. That might sound surprising at first, but according to Grattan, there’s a practical reason this often-overlooked space deserves attention before you walk out the door.

“People tend to focus on storms, break-ins or things happening from the outside,” Grattan says. “But a lot of the biggest problems start inside the home.” And few rooms concentrate more potential for internal problems than the bathroom.

What issues can you potentially prevent by doing this?

A quick photo will help remedy potential problems before you leave and capture a clear “before” state of the room in case something happens while you’re away. Here are things a simple photograph can help prevent and document:

Water damage

At its core, a bathroom is a network of pressurized water lines, drainage systems, seals and fixtures—all operating behind walls and under floors. When everything works, you barely notice it. When something fails, it can fail quickly.

“It’s wall-to-wall plumbing, and plumbing is where a lot of home disasters begin—especially water damage,” Grattan says.

Insurance hassles

Snapping a photo of your bathroom creates a timestamped record if you ever need to document damage for insurance purposes. By providing a clear reference point for the condition of the space before anything went wrong, your photo can help establish what changed and when.

“If you come home and there’s a leak or a stain that wasn’t there before, you’ve got proof of what the room looked like when you left,” Grattan explains.

Expensive replacements

Bathrooms also tend to hold more value than people realize, Grattan says. Beyond the fixtures themselves, there are often high-end items in constant rotation: electric toothbrushes, hair tools, skin-care devices, cosmetics and small appliances that can be easily overlooked in a rush … and would be expensive to replace if ruined.

Bad habits

Every time you snap a photo, you get a sense of your routine and what would work better next time. Maybe you never noticed that you leave your hair dryer plugged in all day until it’s time to do your nightly skin-care routine. Snapping a photo when you’re leaving could prompt you to develop better, safer habits—and unplug that dryer before you walk out the door.

Which areas of your bathroom should you photograph?

Grattan recommends starting with wide shots and then documenting anything of particular value. And there are a few nonnegotiables in terms of what you should do before snapping that ever-important bathroom photo. Here’s what to focus on:

  • The entire bathroom: Snap a photo of the whole room first.
  • The toilet and surrounding floor area: Toilets rely on internal mechanisms to regulate water flow. If a float or valve fails, the system can continue trying to refill indefinitely. “If something goes wrong, it may not shut off on its own,” Grattan says. “And that’s where continuous water flow becomes a real problem.”
  • The vanity and sink area: Unplug everything on the vanity before you snap a photo. Flat irons, hair dryers and electric toothbrushes are fire hazards.
  • Faucets, shower and bathtub: A single dripping faucet can waste more than 3,000 gallons of water per year, according to the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency. If you’re leaving for an extended period, a drip can waste water and run up your bill—or worse, turn into an escalating and damaging leak while you’re away.
  • Walls and ceilings: This is useful if you have cracks that develop or leaks that were lurking undetected before you left.
  • Electronics and high-value personal items: Expensive gadgets, workout equipment, jewelry and clothing are expensive to replace, and photos can help for insurance claims.
  • Cabinets and storage areas: If water or other damage strikes while you’re away, photos of your storage areas can help later.

Other areas of your home to monitor

Laundry rooms and nearby utility areas are other common sources of trouble.

Washing machine hoses remain pressurized even when the machine isn’t running, and over time, those hoses can weaken or fail. “If a hose bursts while you’re away, there’s nothing stopping that water,” Grattan says. “It just keeps going.” For longer trips, he recommends turning off the water supply valves to appliances entirely.

Water heaters are another overlooked risk. If a storage tank fails while a home is empty, the damage can escalate quickly. “You’re talking about 30 to 70 gallons of water released immediately,” Grattan notes. “And if it keeps trying to refill, it just keeps feeding the problem.”

In addition to flooding, hot water can create steam damage in enclosed spaces, leading to lingering moisture, odors and even mold conditions. For added protection, some homeowners install inexpensive water sensors that connect to smartphone apps and alert them to leaks in real time.

How often should you do this?

Taking photos doesn’t need to become part of your everyday exit routine. Instead, it’s most useful anytime you’re leaving for more than 24 hours or anytime you’ll be out of the house long enough that you wouldn’t immediately notice a developing problem.

“I always tell people to take photos or, even better, do a quick video walkthrough,” Grattan says. “A video catches things you might not think to photograph, like subtle leaks, sounds from appliances or the general condition of the space. It gives you a fuller picture if you ever need to compare later.”

Ideally, your photos or videos should live in a dedicated album on your phone—something like “Home Before Trips”—so they’re easy to find if needed, rather than buried in a camera roll. It takes less than a minute. If nothing goes wrong, it’s forgotten instantly. And if something does, it may be one of the most useful photos you’ve ever taken.

About the expert

  • Paul Grattan is a licensed home inspector, emergency preparedness consultant and former NYPD sergeant with decades of experience in public safety and home-risk assessment. He is the founder of Graypoint Strategies and has advised homeowners, businesses and public agencies on disaster preparedness, property safety and emergency response planning.

Why trust us

We rely on reputable primary sources, including government and professional organizations and academic institutions as well as our writers’ personal experiences where appropriate. articles.title: From KPop Demon Hunters to Serious TV Nostalgia, These Are the Biggest Baby-Name Trends of 2026 So Far
articles.content:
谁不喜欢有趣的病毒式传播趋势呢？看看罗马帝国和苏格拉底对决骷髅的梗吧！婴儿名字趋势通常没那么疯狂或随机，但BabyCenter刚刚发布的2026年趋势让我们大吃一惊。事实上，这些2026趋势包罗万象，将全球风格与经典电视节目的怀旧情结相结合，还有那些非常火爆的名字。

为了解释这些超乎常规的婴儿名字，我们求助于BabyCenter该领域专家丽贝卡·瓦尔伯格，她指出这些年中期的分类与2026年初的预测略有不同。当时，NBA球星、牛仔卡特和弗朗西斯（教皇）变体等名字正在婴儿名字领域走红。但在两种情况下，“流行文化都很盛行——它始终吸引着我们的注意力，”她解释道。“因此，它在人们的意识中占据了大量即时思维空间，所以父母观看和聆听的内容在婴儿名字选择中扮演重要角色是合理的。”

准备好了解2026年迄今为止最大的命名趋势了吗？继续往下看！

BabyCenter依靠使用该网站和应用程序的父母提供的大量名字数据来确定2026年最大的命名趋势。过去20年里，数十万新手父母实时分享了他们宝宝的名字，使BabyCenter能够快速把握时代精神，报道那些飙升的名字和可能即将被淘汰的名字。

除了娱乐世界，对过去的怀念也是2026年婴儿名字大趋势的一部分。“流行文化为父母呈现了他们可能从未考虑过的名字，或者让他们重新想起年轻时那些已经从记忆中淡出但仍留存在心底的怀旧名字，”瓦尔伯格解释道。

以下是你需要了解的每个趋势。

这部关于一支秘密拯救世界的K-pop女团的韩国动画似乎势不可挡。正如前几年流行乐团大热一样，现在KPop Demon Hunters成为焦点，瓦尔伯格表示。这一现象在新手父母中大热，他们选择电影中的角色名字（Rumi、Zoey）以及配音演员的名字，如演唱Rumi部分的阿登（Cho）。

“这次KPop Demon Hunters热潮与我们看到在美国蓬勃发展的其他韩国娱乐出口密切相关——想想：2019年的《寄生虫》、Netflix的《鱿鱼游戏》以及BTS和Blackpink等音乐团体，”瓦尔伯格说道。

K-pop热潮的背后支撑是女孩名字Rumi的巨大飞跃，跃升了惊人的714位。以下是KPop Demon Hunters启发的热门名字：

今年还有更多国际风格，因为2026年是中国农历的火马年。寓意火焰或光明或激发能量的名字正在上升。“生肖、占星术和星座对父母来说是巨大的影响因素，”瓦尔伯格说。“看到这些与火相关的名字以更字面的方式呈现出来很有趣。”

Elio意为太阳或阳光，上升了111位。以下是2026年流行的生肖相关名字：

从青年时期喜爱的节目中流媒体观看然后从中选择名字，是一种重温过去的温馨方式。新手父母再次证明“流行文化在婴儿名字上留下了印记”，瓦尔伯格说。目前提供灵感的节目包括《老友记》（Ross飙升756位！）、《高中音乐剧》、《马尔科姆的一家》、《实习医生风云》和《实习医生格蕾》，后者见证了Arizona这个名字飙升了惊人的584位。

婴儿名字中柔和的元音和辅音很有意义，因为疲惫的父母们不断试图轻柔地让小宝宝安静入睡。这个古老的问题与这一趋势完美契合，该趋势专注于包含字母S、F、M、N、L和R的名字，这些字母在发音时没有硬停顿。其中，Milo首次进入前100名，跃升37位，在2026年排名第93位。

看看更多这些（嘘，安静的！）婴儿名字：

唉，总有一些趋势正在退出，这一次是女孩名字以“Ad-”开头（例如，Adele下降343位至第1211名）。虽然很难确切确定为什么一组名字失宠，但瓦尔伯格认为“这些女孩名字的发音可能对当今的父母来说显得过时了。”她补充说，在男孩名字方面，以“-aden”结尾的名字自2024年以来一直在下降。

以下是2026年中期正在降温的“Ad-”女孩名字：

关于专家

在Reader's Digest，我们致力于通过在相关领域具有专业知识和经验的作家制作高质量内容，并咨询相关合格专家。我们依靠政府、专业组织和学术机构等权威主要来源以及作家在适当情况下的个人经历。我们验证所有事实和数据，用可靠来源支持，并随着时间推移进行复查，以确保它们保持准确和最新。了解更多关于我们团队、贡献者和编辑政策的信息。
articles.summary:
Who doesn’t love a fun viral trend? Looking at you, Roman Empire and Socrates vs. Skeleton! No, baby-name trends aren’t usually quite that wild or random, but the ones just released by BabyCenter surprised us. In fact, these 2026 trends have it all, combining global flair and throwbacks to beloved TV shows with names that are kind of on fire.

For help explaining these out-of-the-box baby names, we turned to Rebekah Wahlberg, a specialist on the topic at BabyCenter, who notes these mid-year groupings differ a bit from what was predicted when 2026 began. Back then, NBA stars, Cowboy Carter and Francis (the Pope) variations, among others, were trending for baby names. Still, in both cases, “pop culture is prevalent—it always has our attention,” she explains. “As such, it holds a significant amount of front-of-mind real estate in people’s consciousness, so it makes sense that what parents are watching and listening to plays a big role in their baby-name choices.”

Ready to find out the biggest naming trends of 2026 so far? Read on!

Get Reader’s Digest’s Read Up newsletter for more news, cleaning, humor, travel, tech and fun facts all week long.

How did BabyCenter determine 2026’s biggest naming trends?

BabyCenter determined 2026’s biggest naming trends by relying on extensive name data provided by parents who use the site and app. Over the past 20 years, hundreds of thousands of new moms and dads have volunteered their wee ones’ names in real time, allowing BabyCenter to quickly tap into the zeitgeist to report on the monikers that are soaring and ones that may be headed to the dustbin.

What are the big baby-name trends of 2026 so far?

Along with the world of entertainment, a longing for the past is part of the big baby-name trends of 2026. “Pop culture presents parents with names they might never have previously considered, or it reconnects them with nostalgic names from their youth that had slipped from their minds but held a spot in their hearts,” Wahlberg explains.

Here’s what you need to know about each trend.

KPop Demon Hunters

It seems there’s no stopping this animated sensation from Korea about a K-pop girl group that secretly fights bad guys to save the world. Just as boy bands were a hit in previous years, now it’s all about KPop Demon Hunters, says Wahlberg. This phenomenon is trending big-time among new parents, who are choosing the movie’s character names (Rumi, Zoey) and those of the voice actors, like Arden (Cho), who sings Rumi’s part.

“This KPop Demon Hunters moment is closely tied to the success of other Korean entertainment exports that we’re seeing thrive in the United States—think: 2019’s Parasite, Netflix’s Squid Game and music groups like BTS and Blackpink,” Wahlberg says.

Underlining the K-pop popularity are the huge strides made by the leading girl’s name Rumi, which jumped an incredible 714 spots. These are the top KPop Demon Hunters–inspired names:

  • Rumi
  • Arden
  • Zoey
  • Rei
  • Jinu
  • Celine

The Year of the Fire Horse

There’s even more international flair this year, as 2026 is the Year of the Fire Horse on the Chinese zodiac calendar. Names that mean fire or light or that evoke energy are on the upswing. “Zodiac, astrology and star signs are huge influences for parents,” says Wahlberg. “It’s fun to see that play out in a more literal way with these fire-associated names.”

Elio, which means sun or sunlight, is up 111 spots. Here are the zodiac-inspired names trending in 2026:

  • Phoenix
  • Apollo
  • Elio
  • Ember
  • Sol
  • Blaze
  • Marisol

Y2K nostalgia from the small screen

Streaming a favorite show from one’s youth and then picking a name from it is a cozy way to revisit the past. And new parents are proving again that “pop culture leaves its stamp on baby names,” says Wahlberg. Among the shows providing ideas right now are Friends (Ross soared 756 places!), High School Musical, Malcolm in the Middle, Scrubs and Grey’s Anatomy, which saw the name Arizona climb a whopping 584 spots.

  • Monica
  • Ross
  • Gabriella
  • Troy
  • Zeke
  • Carla
  • Reese
  • Lois
  • Callie
  • Derek
  • Miranda
  • Arizona

Whisper-soft names

Baby names with quiet vowels and consonants make sense, as weary parents are constantly trying to softly shush their little ones to sleep. This age-old issue dovetails nicely with this trend that focuses on names with the letters S, F, M, N, L and R, which don’t have a hard stop when vocalized. One in particular, Milo, has cracked the top 100 for the first time, jumping 37 spots to land at No. 93 in 2026.

Check out some more of these (shh, quiet!) baby names:

  • Milo
  • Zamir
  • Alonso
  • Amos
  • Lennon
  • Solana
  • Rhea
  • Rosalina
  • Aura
  • Ines
  • Ariel

Which trends are on their way out?

Alas, there’s always a trend or two that’s on the way out, and this time around, it’s girl names that begin with “Ad-” (Adele, for example, dropped 343 places to No. 1,211). Although it’s hard to pin down exactly why a set of names falls out of favor, Wahlberg posits that “the sound in [these] girl names may feel dated for parents today.” On the boys’ side, names ending with “-aden” have been dropping in popularity since 2024, she adds.

Here are the “Ad-” girl monikers that are cooling off in mid-2026:

  • Adele
  • Ada
  • Adelyn
  • Adalee
  • Adira
  • Adilene
  • Adina
  • Adhara

RELATED:

  • A New Royal Baby Is on the Way! Here’s Who’s Expecting—And When We Can Expect the Cute Addition
  • You Won’t Believe How Much a Babysitter Costs Right Now—How Do Your Area’s Rates Compare?
  • It Costs *What* to Raise a Child in 2026?! (And This Doesn’t Even Include College!)

About the expert

  • Rebekah Wahlberg is a specialist in baby-name trends and a senior associate editor at BabyCenter, where this analysis was conducted. She previously worked at the Salt Lake Tribune, a Pulitzer Prize–winning nonprofit newspaper, where she covered breaking news.

Why trust us

At Reader’s Digest, we’re committed to producing high-quality content by writers with expertise and experience in their field in consultation with relevant, qualified experts. We rely on reputable primary sources, including government and professional organizations and academic institutions as well as our writers’ personal experiences where appropriate. We verify all facts and data, back them with credible sourcing and revisit them over time to ensure they remain accurate and up to date. Read more about our team, our contributors and our editorial policies.

Sources:

  • Rebekah Wahlberg, specialist in baby-name trends at BabyCenter; interviewed, June 2026
  • BabyCenter: “The biggest baby name trends of 2026 so far”

The Most Popular Baby Names in Each State

This Baby-Naming Trend Flips the Script

Baby Names for the Season of Rebirth

The post From <i>KPop Demon Hunters</i> to Serious TV Nostalgia, These Are the Biggest Baby-Name Trends of 2026 So Far appeared first on Reader's Digest.

articles.title: Chilipad Review: This Smart Bed Cooling System Is My Secret to Deep Sleep in Summer
articles.content:
在我人生的大部分时间里，我都能享受到甜美、舒适的睡眠。一旦过了30岁，一切都变了。突然间，我再也无法在夜间自主调节体温了。几乎是一夜之间，我从酣畅淋漓的休息睡眠，变成了半夜掀掉被汗水浸透的床单、半夜起来换睡衣。每天早上醒来，我都处于脱水状态，迷迷糊糊，拼命寻找解脱。

我尝试了各种方法：凉感床单、凉感毯子，你能想到的都试了。虽然这些在非夏季月份还算管用，但在闷热的天气里对我毫无帮助。最终，我开始寻找专业的床上降温系统。一款名叫Sleep.me Chilipad的产品引起了我的注意。躺在由冷却水循环管道组成的网络上，这个想法听起来像是科幻小说里的东西，但当时我已经愿意尝试任何方法了。

各位朋友，我真的很庆幸自己迈出了这一步。以下是我测试Chilipad的经历——以及为什么我将成为它一辈子的用户。

Chilipad睡眠系统使用充满水的水循环层，确保睡眠者整夜保持凉爽。

Chilipad前身为Chilipad Dock Pro，是一款为怕热睡眠者设计的巧妙解决方案。Chilipad由几个部分组成，包括一个主机（看起来有点像我们家的投影仪）、软管、床垫罩，以及——重头戏——水循环层插入件。

这些部件连接在一起形成了一个床垫护垫。护垫内布满冷却水通道网络，这些通道会将你身体的热量吸收到水中。然后，水被泵回主机进行冷却，再循环回到水循环层继续制冷。

我最初是在2025年夏天测试了原版Chilipad Dock Pro。最近，我收到了Chilipad 2.0，这是Sleep.me床上降温系统的最新一代。操作方式大体相同，但2.0版本相比原版Dock Pro增加了一些新功能。在这篇文章中，我将重点介绍2.0版本，因为Dock Pro目前正在逐步淘汰。

当我第一次打开Chilipad的包装时，看到所有这些连接器和配件，我有点吓到了。然而，按照附带的指南，第一次运行却出奇地简单。以下是基本步骤：

然后，主机屏幕会提示你加入一整包系统清洁剂和蒸馏水，直到“低水位”图标消失。

设置完成后，我将遥控旋钮调到首选温度73华氏度。主机立即开始冷却水并将其泵入水循环层。虽然不是完全静音，但噪音水平低到不会影响我睡觉。然而，当需要加水时，主机和水循环层泵的噪音都会变大。

你知道那种滑入冰凉床单时的美妙感觉吗？使用Chilipad就像是整晚都能享受那种感觉。需要一两晚来适应身下水流动的感觉，但经过一段时间的调整后，我觉得这相当舒缓。

去年整个夏天我都使用原版Chilipad Dock Pro，在过去一个月里几乎每晚都使用新的Chilipad 2.0。令人难以置信的是，使用这个设备期间，我没有一个晚上出过汗。在一些较凉爽的夏夜，我曾尝试不打开Chilipad睡觉。但每次都以睡衣湿透告终。对我来说，Chilipad绝对是获得良好睡眠的必备品。

每台Chilipad都附带几包系统清洁剂让你开始使用。总的来说，这个设备也出乎意料地易于维护。当主机屏幕上出现低水位图标时，我会用蒸馏水补充。每隔一个月，加入一包系统清洁剂以防止细菌滋生。你还需要每三到六个月对主机进行一次“深度清洁”，方法是排空并重新加水。

最新版本的Chilipad 2.0提供了床垫保护套和床边“遥控器”，这是相比Dock Pro最大的改进。这些小调整对整体体验产生了重大影响。Dock Pro的水循环层自带一层覆盖物，而不是2.0版本的拉链式全床保护套。两根弹性带从水循环层顶部延伸到底部，滑到床垫下面固定住所有部件。虽然功能正常，但相比新版本确实更容易移动，新版本可以牢牢扣在床垫保护套里面。

有了新的遥控旋钮，就不需要打开应用程序来调节温度了。床垫保护套在你和水循环层之间创造了一层舒适的隔离层。由于顶部和底部有可调节的夹子，它也能更好地适应各种高度的床垫。这层更厚的材质让我对可能发生的穿刺或漏水更加放心。不过，我仍然不让我的猫上床，以防它们用爪子刺破Chilipad。

虽然我仍在长期测试新的Chilipad 2.0，但去年夏天使用过的原版Chilipad Dock Pro仍然运作良好。我把它设在我丈夫那边的床上，即使存放了六个月，一切仍然运行顺畅。只需要一包新的系统清洁剂就能重新启动。

不过，如果你打算在不同季节之间收纳起来，存放并不是特别简单。首先，你必须排空机器内部的存水，否则水和细菌会损坏主机内部。然后，你不仅要存放这个垫子（这已经相当困难了），还要找到空间存放主机本身。而主机相当大且重，所以需要一个专门的空间。

展望未来，我计划整年都把两台Chilipad放在我们的床上。虽然冬天我不会用我那台，但我丈夫很容易怕冷，他打算用原版Dock Pro来保持床的温暖。

我们的产品测试人员也试用过Eight Sleep Pod 3（现在已有第五代）和Eight Sleep床垫Pod Cover，以及Bedjet制冷系统。虽然这些选择都运作良好，但它们之间有许多关键区别。以下是它们的快速对比：

作为一个饱受夜间盗汗困扰的人，能够整夜安睡不仅仅是舒适——而是具有改变人生的意义。Chilipad给我带来的解脱确实提高了我的生活质量。是的，我现在平均睡眠时间更长了，但根据我的睡眠追踪器的数据，睡眠质量也更好了。早上醒来神清气爽，而不是无精打采地从床上爬起来，这让我一整天都有积极的影响。最棒的是什么？不需要每月订阅费！

如果你发现自己因为床太热而辗转反侧，在床上制冷系统上花大钱，比如Chilipad，会带来天壤之别的体验。没错，它价格不菲，但睡眠质量的改善绝对是无价的。此外，它还创造了一个更干净、更轻松的睡眠环境。我没有夸张，当我以前每天晚上都要洗床单。使用Chilipad以来，我再也没有发生过夜间盗汗。一次都没有！

对于经常夜间盗汗的人来说，Chilipad 2.0就像是为你的床垫配备了一台私人空调。

如果你想永远告别夜间盗汗，Chilipad 2.0和Dock Pro都可以在Sleep.me的网站上购买。2.0套装——包括遥控器、主机、水循环层和床垫罩——根据床的尺寸，单面售价为1500到1800美元。虽然价格不菲（对于需要为另一睡眠者提供全床覆盖的人来说更是如此），但没有订阅模式意味着你只需一次性支付固定费用就能获得所有功能。

虽然你仍然可以以略微折扣的价格购买Dock Pro，但它目前正在逐步淘汰，所以我建议投资新版本以获得最佳体验。

在整个去年夏天以及今年夏天测试了Chilipad之后，我可以高兴地报告，它确实有效！

某种床上制冷系统是否比另一种更好，主要取决于你的个人偏好。我们测试过Eight Sleep和Chilipad，发现它们都是控制夜间盗汗的有效工具。

不！Chilipad是少数不需要每月订阅的床上制冷系统之一。虽然你可以下载配套应用程序，但并不是必需的。

是的，你需要在Chilipad中使用蒸馏水。

作为《读者文摘》、《美食之家》和《家庭巧手》杂志的购物编辑，价值始终是我最优先考虑的。我比任何人都清楚，并非所有产品都能兑现承诺。这就是为什么我在自己家里测试了一年的Chilipad才起草这篇评测。我也是一个怕热的人——我尝试过一切方法来解决这个问题。在使用过原版Chilipad Dock Pro和新款Chilipad 2.0之后，我可以放心地说，这个品牌为像我这样的人提供了非常可靠的床上制冷系统。

这篇文章首次发表于《读者文摘》：Chilipad评测：这款智能床上制冷系统是我在夏天深度睡眠的秘密武器
articles.summary:
For most of my life, I’ve enjoyed blissful, refreshing sleep. Once I hit 30, things changed. Suddenly, I could no longer self-regulate my temperature at night. Almost overnight, I went from glorious, restful sleep to ripping off my sweat-soaked sheets and changing my pajamas in the middle of the night. I woke up each morning dehydrated, confused and desperately searching for relief.

I tried everything: cooling sheets, blankets, you name it. While they worked fine for the non-summer months, they did nothing for me in sweltering temperatures. Eventually, I began looking at proper bed cooling systems. One specific product, known as the Sleep.me Chilipad, caught my eye. The prospect of lying on a network of cooling water tunnels sounded like something out of science fiction, but I was willing to try anything by that point.

And folks, I’m so glad I made the leap. Here’s my experience testing the Chilipad—and why I’m a customer for life.

We Tried It

Chilipad 2.0 Bed Cooling System

Using a water-filled hydrolayer, the Chilipad sleep system ensures sleepers stay cool throughout the night.

Shop on Sleep.me

What is the Chilipad?

Formerly called the Chilipad Dock Pro, the Chilipad 2.0 is a clever solution for overheated snoozers. The Chilipad comes in several parts, including a dock (which looks a bit like our family projector), hose, mattress cover and—the pièce de résistance—the hydrolayer insert.

Together, these pieces connect to create a mattress topper. The topper contains a network of cooling water channels, and these channels pull the heat from your body to the water. Then, the water is pumped back into the dock to cool down, where it’s then recycled back into the hydrolayer for further cooling magic.

How we tested it

I originally tested the original Chilipad Dock Pro in the summer of 2025. Recently, I received the Chilipad 2.0, which is the newest generation of Sleep.me’s bed cooling system. The operation is largely the same, though the 2.0 offers a few new bells and whistles compared to the original Dock Pro. For this piece, I’ll focus on the 2.0, since the Dock Pro is currently being phased out.

When I first unpacked the Chilipad, I was a little intimidated by all the connectors and gizmos. However, running it for the first time is surprisingly easy, especially using the included guide. Here are the basic steps:

  1. Strip your bed of everything, including mattress protectors
  2. Tuck the mattress cover over the mattress and unzip it
  3. Fit the hydrolayer inside
  4. Fasten the clips on the top and bottom of both the hydrolayer and mattress protector so both stay securely in place
  5. Locate the water hose opening in the mattress cover and feed the hose through
  6. Connect both ends of the hose to the hydrolayer and dock
  7. Zip up the cover and make the bed
  8. Plug the dock’s power cord into an electrical outlet
  9. Plug the remote dial into an electrical outlet
  10. Power on the dock and the dial

From there, the dock screen will prompt you to add a full packet of system cleaner and distilled water until the ‘low water’ icon disappears.

Once set up, I turned the remote dial to my preferred 73ºF. The dock immediately gets to work cooling the water and pumping it into the hydrolayer. While it’s not entirely silent, the noise level is low enough not to disturb me as I sleep. However, both the dock and hydrolayer pump get noisier when it’s about time to add more water.

You know that wonderful feeling when you slip between two cold sheets? Having the Chilipad is like enjoying that feeling all night long. It takes a night or two to get used to the sensation of the water moving below you, but after some adjustment, I found it rather soothing.

I used my original Chilipad Dock Pro throughout last summer, and my new Chilipad 2.0 nearly every single night during the last month. Incredibly, I haven’t had a single night sweat while using this device. During some chillier summer nights I have tried sleeping without my Chilipad turned on. But I ended up humbled each time by sweaty pajamas. For me, the Chilipad is absolutely essential for a good night’s sleep.

Cleaning

Each Chilipad comes with several packages of system cleaner to get you started. Overall, the device is also surprisingly low maintenance. I top it up with distilled water when the low water icon appears on the dock. Every month, just add a packet of system cleaner to keep bacteria at bay. You’ll also want to “deep clean” the dock by draining and refilling it every three to six months.

Pros

  • Provides instant relief for hot sleepers
  • Super easy to use and install
  • No subscription required
  • With the remote, you don’t need the app (but it’s nice to have)
  • The two-layer system offers a built-in mattress protector
  • 3-year warranty included

Cons

  • Not loud, but also not silent
  • Pricey—especially for full bed systems
  • Requires space for the dock

Original Chilipad Dock Pro

The newest Chilipad 2.0 version offers a mattress protector and bedside “remote,” which are the biggest changes since the Dock Pro. These small adjustments make a big impact on the overall experience. The Dock Pro’s hydrolayer has a built-in cover, rather than the 2.0’s zipped full bed protector. Two elastic bands run from the top of the hydrolayer to the bottom and slide under the mattress, keeping everything in place. While functional, it does move around more than the new version, which clips securely into place inside of the mattress protector.

With the new remote dial, there’s no need to fire up your app for temperature changes. The mattress protector creates a comfortable layer between you and the hydrolayer. It also allows for better adjustment to a variety of mattress heights thanks to adjustable clips on the top and bottom. This thicker layer gives me more peace of mind against any punctures or leaks that could occur. That said, I still don’t let my cats onto the bed in case they pierce the Chilipad with their claws.

Long-term testing

While I’m still in the process of testing the new Chilipad 2.0 long term, the original Chilipad Dock Pro I used last summer still works well. I set it up on my husband’s side of the bed and everything still runs smoothly, even after six months of storage. It just required a new packet of system cleaner to get up and running.

That said, storage isn’t super straightforward if you plan on packing it up between seasons. First, you have to drain the machine, lest water and bacteria harm the interior of the dock. Not only do you then have to store the pad (which is already quite difficult), but you also have to find space to store the unit itself. And the unit is pretty sizable and heavy, so it requires a dedicated space.

Going forward, I plan on keeping both Chilipads on our bed year-round. While I won’t use mine much in the winter, my husband gets cold very easily and plans on using the original Dock Pro to keep his side of the bed warm.

Product comparison: Chilipad vs. Bedjet vs. Eight Sleep

Our product testers have also tried the Eight Sleep Pod 3 (there’s now a fifth generation) and the Eight Sleep mattress Pod Cover, along with the Bedjet cooling system. While these options all work well, there are many key differences between them. Here’s how they compare at a glance:

  • The Bedjet, unlike Eight Sleep and the Chilipad, uses air rather than water for cooling
  • The Bedjet is the most budget-friendly, but also the noisiest
  • Eight Sleep products require a monthly subscription to access all the features—they’re also the priciest option
  • While there’s an app for both the Bedjet and Chilipad, they’re not required for use
  • Unlike Chilipad and Bedjet, Eight Sleep’s cooling systems also come with trackers to gather sleep data
  • All options have a clunky dock that might not fit under the bed

Final verdict

As someone who suffers from night sweats, being able to sleep peacefully throughout the night isn’t just nice—it’s transformative. The relief I get from my Chilipad has genuinely improved my quality of life. Yes, I get more sleep on average now, but according to my sleep tracker it’s also better quality sleep. Waking up refreshed rather than sluggishly pulling myself out of bed each morning affects my whole day in a positive way. And the best part? There’s no monthly subscription needed!

If you find yourself tossing and turning from an overly warm bed, splurging on a bed cooling system like the Chilipad makes a night and day difference. Sure, it’s pricey, but the improvement in sleep quality is absolutely priceless. Plus, it makes for a much cleaner and less stressful sleep environment. I am not exaggerating when I say that I used to wash my sheets every night. Since using the Chilipad, I’ve had zero instances of night sweats. Zero!

Where to buy the Chilipad

We Tried It

Chilipad 2.0 Bed Cooling System

For folks with frequent night sweats, the Chilipad 2.0 is like having a personal air conditioner for your mattress.

Shop on Sleep.me

If you want to say goodbye to the night sweats for good, the Chilipad 2.0 and Dock Pro are both available on Sleep.me’s website. The 2.0 bundle—which includes the remote, dock, hydrolayer and mattress cover—retails for $1,500 to $1,800 for a single side, depending on the size of the bed. While pricey (especially for those who require full bed coverage for a second sleeper), the lack of a subscription model means that you only have to pay a single flat fee for all the features.

While you can still snag the Dock Pro at a slight discount, it’s currently being phased out, so I recommend investing in the newer version for the best experience.

FAQ

Does Chilipad really work?

After testing the Chilipad over last summer and into this summer, I can happily report that it does indeed work!

Is Eight Sleep better than Chilipad?

Whether one bed cooling system is better than another largely depends on your personal preferences. We’ve tested both Eight Sleep and Chilipad and find them both useful tools for managing night sweats.

Does Chilipad require a subscription?

Nope! Chilipad is one of the only bed cooling systems that doesn’t require a monthly subscription. While you can download the companion app, it isn’t required.

Do I need distilled water for the Chilipad?

Yes, you need to use distilled water in the Chilipad.

Why you should trust us

As a Shopping Editor at Reader’s Digest, Taste of Home and Family Handyman, value is always my top priority. I know better than anyone that not all products deliver on their promises. That’s why I tested my Chilipad for a year in my own home before drafting up this review. I’m also a hot sleeper—and I’ve tried everything to fix it. After having used both the original Chilipad Dock Pro and the new Chilipad 2.0, I can safely say that the brand offers some seriously solid bed cooling systems for folks like me.

The post Chilipad Review: This Smart Bed Cooling System Is My Secret to Deep Sleep in Summer appeared first on Reader's Digest.

articles.title: Fall Jellycats Are Coming—And Just Wait Until You See the S’mores!
articles.content:
又一个季节，又一款Jellycat新品！虽然天气终于开始转暖，但Jellycat团队已经在为凉爽的月份做准备了。2026年秋季系列将于秋季前上市，带来九位新伙伴，全都散发着有趣的森林气息。

如果你和我们一样，可能已经在计划提前购买了——6月16日发售日可千万别错过。与Jellycat家族的其他成员一样，这些以自然为灵感的小伙伴们看起来非常适合拥抱——看看那标志性的毛绒面料就知道了！

与夏季系列一样，我们预计这些产品会很快售罄。如果你爱上了某款Jellycat，记得在发售日当天就把它加入购物车！

这批最新的秋季Jellycat系列以植物细节为亮点，可爱的毛绒小家伙们正在拥抱大自然。这批新Jellies以森林为主题的朋友为特色，比如远足的巴塞洛缪熊和花生雪人。甚至还有一对甜蜜的棉花糖巧克力搭档！我们询问了购物编辑们的最爱。继续阅读，看看我们的精选推荐！

闲话少说，以下是新品完整名单：

谈到Jellycat，我们不得不提到巴塞洛缪熊。他全新的远足装扮包括一条粗犷的工装短裤和一件以森林为灵感设计的毛衣，让他看起来充满冒险精神。我们还喜欢他附带的背包，可以变成睡袋供小憩之用！

罗克顿是我个人最喜欢的新品。这个几何小子有一张甜美、好奇的脸，让你忍不住想给他一个大大的拥抱。而且——尽管他看起来像石头做的——他仍然和你其他喜爱的Jellies一样柔软舒适。身高超过一英尺，他在森林里漫步时确实很难被忽视！

“虽然夏天才刚开始，但多亏了这些Amuseables橡果，我已经迫不及待想到秋天了，”Jellycat专家兼副主编凯蒂·班杜尔斯基说道。“这对活力搭档是秋天的完美象征，我喜欢它们身上使用的各种面料质感。它们太可爱了，我简直能想象奥尔特和塔姆出现在我的感恩节餐桌布置上！”

“我还没有Jellycat（目前没有），”作家芭芭拉·齐托说。“但棉花糖巧克力已经俘获了我的心。我完全能想象这对可爱小家伙待在我的架子上。它们和真的一样甜！”

“我不确定Amuseables穆尔希林地是什么吸引了我，但我觉得我必须拥有它，”作家玛丽莎·希尔曼说。“它让我想起夏令营的日子，以及那些每天被我装进背包的自然宝藏。”

一如既往，你可以直接在Jellycat官网找到新的秋季Jellycats。大多数款式将于6月16日开始发售，但我们认为它们不会在货架上停留太久！如果你爱上了某款Jellycat，建议你尽快入手。

一如既往：小心Jellycat仿冒品。许多第三方卖家用正品的图片来销售假冒毛绒玩具。如果你看到某款产品的价格明显低于Jellycat的标价，好得令人难以置信时，那很可能就是假的。

这篇文章《秋季Jellycats即将到来——等你看到棉花糖巧克力就知道了！》首次发表于《读者文摘》。
articles.summary:
Another season, another Jellycat collection! While the weather may have finally started to warm up, the Jellycat team has been thinking ahead to the cooler months. Dropping ahead of autumn, the fall 2026 Jellycat collection brings with it nine new pals, all with a fun forest vibe.

If you’re anything like us, you’re already planning your purchases ahead of the June 16 launch. Like the rest of the Jellycat family, these nature-inspired pals look positively cozy to cuddle—just check out that fuzzy signature fabric!

As with the Summer Jellycat collection, we expect these to sell out fast. If you fall in love with a specific Jelly, plan to pop it in your cart on launch day!

What are the fall Jellycats?

Boasting botanical details, the cuddly critters from the newest fall Jellycat collection are channeling nature. This new crop of Jellies features forest-themed friends like a hiking Bartholomew and a peanut yeti. There’s even a sweet little s’mores duo! We polled our shopping editors for their favorites. Read on to see our top picks!

Without further ado, here’s the full roll call of new Jellies:

  • Amuseables Zapperty Lightning Bolt
  • Bretti Yeti
  • Amuseables S’mores
  • Amuseables Mulshi Woodland Floor
  • Amuseables Ort & Tum Acorns
  • Rockleton
  • Bartholomew Bear Hiking Outfit
  • Amuseables Peanut Yeti Outfit
  • Trixiwoo Raccoon

Our top picks

Bartholomew Bear Hiking Outfit

Shop on Jellycat

When discussing Jellycats, we simply have to mention Bartholomew Bear. His new hiking outfit, comprised of a pair of rugged cargo shorts and a forest-inspired sweater, gives him an adventurous look. We also love that he comes with a backpack that converts into a sleeping bag for snoozing!

Rockleton

Shop on Jellycat

Rockleton is my personal favorite from the new collection. This geo-dude has a sweet, curious face that makes you want to give him a big hug. And—despite his rocky appearance—he’s still as soft and cozy as your other favorite Jellies. Standing at over a foot tall, it’s certainly difficult to miss him as he roams the woods!

Amuseables Ort & Tum Acorns

Shop on Jellycat

“Even though summer just started, I’m already thinking ahead to autumn thanks to these Amuseables Acorns” says resident Jellycat expert and Deputy Editor Katie Bandurski. “This dynamic duo is the perfect symbol for fall, and I love the variety of fabric textures used in their construction. They’re so cute, I could see Ort & Tum making an appearance on my Thanksgiving tablescape!”

Amuseables S'mores

Shop on Jellycat

“I don’t have any Jellycats (yet),” says writer Barbara Zito. “But the s’mores have won my heart. I could totally see this pair of cuties hanging out on my shelf. They’re as sweet as the real thing!”

Amuseables Mulshi Woodland Floor

Shop on Jellycat

“I’m not sure what it is about the Amuseables Mulshi Woodland Floor, but I feel like I have to have it,” says writer Marisa Hillman. “It reminds me of my summer camp days and the collection of nature treasures that made it into my backpack each day.”

Where to buy

As always, you can find the newfall Jellycats directly on the Jellycat website. Most styles will be available starting June 16, but we don’t think they’ll stick around for long! If you fall in love with a specific Jelly, we recommend snagging it as soon as you can.

As always: Be careful of Jellycat knock-offs. Many third-party sellers list faux plush using images of the real deal. If you see a price that is significantly lower than the list price through Jellycat, it’s likely too good to be true.

The post Fall Jellycats Are Coming—And Just Wait Until You See the S’mores! appeared first on Reader's Digest.
